---
title: "PAM"
author: "Jill Ashey"
date: "2026-05-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
```

Read in list of tiff files 
```{r}
tiff <- read.csv("../data/Physiology/PAM/tiff_file_list_20260511.csv", header = F)
```

Read in results 
```{r}
pam_res <- read.csv("../data/Physiology/PAM/Results.csv", header = T)
pam_res <- pam_res[-1,]
```

```{r}
# Map pam results to sample names 
pam_mapped <- pam_res %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_Name = rep(tiff$V1, each = 6, length.out = n()),
    # Create an index (1 to 6) for each row within the sample group
    Layer_Index = rep(1:6, length.out = n())
  )

# Calculate Fv/Fm
final_results <- pam_mapped %>%
  # 1. Filter only the rows we need
  filter(Layer_Index %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Sample_Name, Layer_Index, Mean) %>%
  
  # 2. Pivot (this creates the list-cols and the warning)
  pivot_wider(names_from = Layer_Index, names_prefix = "Layer_", values_from = Mean) %>%
  
  # 3. FIX: Convert the lists back into normal numeric columns
  unnest(cols = c(Layer_1, Layer_2)) %>%
  
  # 4. Filter out the specific file you don't want
  filter(Sample_Name != "12_A3_pHi.tif") %>%
  
  # 5. Now the math will work perfectly!
  mutate(
    Fv_Fm = (Layer_2 - Layer_1) / Layer_2
  )
  

# View results
head(final_results)

final_results$SampleID <- final_results$Sample_Name
```

Format sample names for easy join 
```{r}
final_results$SampleID <- gsub(".tif", "", final_results$SampleID)
final_results$SampleID <- gsub("PHI", "pHi", final_results$SampleID)
```

Merge with sample info 
```{r}
meta <- read.csv("../data/sample_manifest.csv", header = T)
meta$SampleID <- gsub("PC-", "", meta$SampleID)
meta$SampleID <- gsub("MC-", "", meta$SampleID)

final_results <- final_results %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID") %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Fv_Fm, Species, Phenotype, Treatment)
```

Plot! 
```{r}
# Filter out rows where Species or Treatment might be NA to keep the plot clean
plot_data <- final_results[!is.na(final_results$Species) & !is.na(final_results$Treatment), ]

ggplot(plot_data, aes(y = Fv_Fm, color = Phenotype, x = NA)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +
  # Use vars() to wrap by both Species and Treatment
  facet_wrap(vars(Species, Treatment)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Photosynthetic Efficiency (Fv/Fm) by Species and Treatment",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = expression(F[v]/F[m]),
    color = "Phenotype"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
